metric_value: Focusing on core competencies: decision that IT excellence is best achieved by an outsider
notes: First of five ITO drivers tracked by Aberdeen since 2004.

metric_value: Saving money: most expected benefit; palming off dysfunctional IT on naïve outsourcer is a pipe dream
notes: Second ITO driver. Kastner's candid assessment of unrealistic cost-saving expectations.

metric_value: IT resource constraints: lack of talent, costly talent, staff turnover, wrong skill-sets
Time-to-market concern in highly competitive industries where IT agility is critical
notes: Fourth ITO driver. Outsourcer size, skills, agility often cited.

metric_value: Culture wars: LOB management fed up with IT intransigence; outsourcing as 'blow it up' nuclear option
notes: Fifth ITO driver. Kastner's characterization of organizational conflict as outsourcing catalyst.

metric_value: Up to 30% of IT outsourcing engagements seriously misaligned; customers may not realize it
notes: Kastner hypothesis to be tested in June 2006 benchmark. Misalignment from scope ambiguity, inaccurate metrics, business change.

metric_value: 50-67% of IT outsourcing contracts running at sub-optimal levels
notes: Kastner hypothesis: majority of ITO engagements underperform. Range 50-67%.

metric_value: Only 5-10% of multi-million dollar IT outsourcing agreements drive all anticipated business value
notes: Kastner hypothesis: elite minority of ITO contracts deliver on full business value promise.

metric_value: 50% of Best-in-Class measure ROI after every application change vs. 25% for Laggards
notes: Figure 1 data. Source: AberdeenGroup, Achieving More Value from Enterprise Integration, May 2006.
Aberdeen IT maturity model distribution from Figure 1.

metric_value: Poor choices in metrics and SLAs, confusing low cost with value delivered, inflexible contract language
notes: Outsourcing customer complaints leading to buyer's remorse.

metric_value: Aberdeen tracking IT planning and outsourcing issues quantitatively since 2004
notes: Establishes Aberdeen's longitudinal tracking baseline for ITO research.

metric_value: Over 2,000 companies surveyed on IT in past two years
notes: Aberdeen ITO research data foundation as of May 2006.

metric_value: Many enterprises have never done a serious make-or-buy outsourcing decision on any IT segment
notes: Kastner research question: what inhibits enterprises from seriously evaluating IT outsourcing?

metric_value: Cannot name a single company that has outsourced IT and not learned painful lessons
notes: Kastner's universal observation on ITO learning curve.

metric_value: SLAs, governance, preparation, transition, operations, change management, pricing, conflict resolution, metrics
notes: Nine dimensions Aberdeen will probe to assess ITO sub-optimality.

metric_value: Report will benchmark all-size companies in Global 5000 across all industries
notes: Planned scope of June 2006 Aberdeen ITO benchmark report.
